title: settings — declarative attributes
description: Three PHP attributes drive discovery + rendering. Grouped as the platform's public authoring API.

attributes.name: AsSetting
attributes.purpose: Marks a settings class for registry discovery. One per Settings subclass.

arguments.name: group
arguments.description: Unique group key. Matches API route segment + spatie group identifier.

arguments.name: label
arguments.description: Human display label. Empty falls back to the group key.

arguments.name: permission
arguments.description: Permission gating GET; PUT always requires settings.update.{group}.

arguments.name: public
arguments.description: Whether GET skips auth. PUT still requires permission.

attributes.name: SettingGroup
attributes.purpose: Defines a visual section within a settings class. Other #[SettingField] on the same class reference this section via their `group` argument.

arguments.name: label
arguments.description: Display label AND reference key. Fields opt in via field.group === section.label.

attributes.name: SettingField
attributes.purpose: Declares a single field's control type + validation + display metadata.

arguments.name: label
arguments.description: Empty falls back to snake_case property → Title Case.

arguments.name: validation
arguments.description: Laravel validation rules applied server-side before writing.

arguments.name: defaultValue
arguments.description: Escape-hatch when the property default can't express it (closures, computed shapes).

arguments.name: group
arguments.description: References a #[SettingGroup] label — attaches the field to that visual section.

arguments.name: options
arguments.description: Enum FQCN, associative value=>label, or flat list.

arguments.name: sensitive
arguments.description: Masked in reads unless caller has settings.view-sensitive.

conventions: Every settings class MUST declare `#[AsSetting(group: '<unique-key>')]` — duplicate group keys fail the compile-time discovery pass.
Fields WITHOUT a `#[SettingField]` attribute are not exposed via HTTP (they still persist via spatie).
Sections are optional. A settings class with no #[SettingGroup] renders fields in a single implicit 'General' section.
